objective: Make /srv/monorepo the functional control plane for the Ubuntu Desktop homelab, with Hermes agents executing through typed CLI/API adapters and a small canonical spec set.

phases.name: Intake and Canonical Specs
phases.gate: SPEC-001..004 exist and ACTIVE.md points to them

phases.name: Security and Drift Cleanup
phases.gate: No real secrets in docs/tasks and runtime drift list recorded

phases.name: Hermes Control Runtime
phases.gate: homelab-control skill and CLI adapters implemented

phases.name: Memory RAG LLM Consistency
phases.gate: embedding dimension and health checks consistent

phases.name: Verification and Ship
phases.gate: checks pass or Gitea issue opened with exact blockers

queue.title: Redact real secrets from specs/tasks/docs
acceptance: No real secret values in Markdown or task JSON
Docs reference env var names only

queue.title: Canonicalize active SPECs to SPEC-001..004
acceptance: Legacy specs are classified as historical or implementation notes

queue.title: Repair Plan Mode Phase 0
acceptance: No placeholder JSON vector syntax remains
Phase 0 either uses real embeddings or logs an explicit degraded skip

queue.title: Normalize Hermes port docs
acceptance: Hermes Gateway 8642 and Hermes Agency 3001 are not conflated

queue.title: Add queue runner for tasks/pipeline.json
acceptance: One command claims the next queued task
Result is written back to pipeline state

queue.title: Add RAG health endpoint/check
acceptance: Health output shows Qdrant, Trieve, Ollama embedding, and Mem0 degraded/ok state

notes: Old SPEC-090 3-phase model is retained as a useful idea, not active implementation truth.
Old SPEC-120/121/122 are incident notes; do not copy secrets or runtime values from them.
Control plane agents should use typed CLI/API adapters, not untracked shell prompts.
